lede: A look at November 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tiberius and William Bonner (newscaster), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2002–2004 SARS outbreak
events.desc: The first cases of the 2002–2004 SARS outbreak are traced to Foshan, Guangdong Province, China.

events.title: China
events.desc: After nearly 18 years of incarceration, China releases Wei Jingsheng, a pro-democracy dissident, from jail for medical reasons.

events.title: Pakistan
events.desc: In the first open election in more than a decade, voters in Pakistan elect populist candidate Benazir Bhutto to be Prime Minister of Pakistan.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: U.S. president Richard Nixon signs the Trans-Alaska Pipeline Authorization Act into law, authorizing the construction of the Alaska Pipeline.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 2230
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 2230 crashes near Koltsovo Airport, killing 107.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In response to the leveling of Coventry by the German Luftwaffe two days before, the Royal Air Force bombs Hamburg.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: In occupied Poland, the Nazis close off the Warsaw Ghetto from the outside world.

events.title: Fyodor Dostoevsky
events.desc: A Russian court sentences writer Fyodor Dostoyevsky to death for anti-government activities linked to a radical intellectual group; his sentence is later commuted to hard labor.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British and Hessian units capture Fort Washington from the Patriots.

events.title: Gustavus Adolphus
events.desc: King Gustavus Adolphus of Sweden was killed at the Battle of Lützen during the Thirty Years' War.

births.name: José Saramago
births.line: Portuguese novelist and Nobel laureate in Literature

births.name: Andrea Barrett
births.line: American novelist and short story writer

deaths.text: Milton Friedman, American economist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Gertrude the Great (Roman Catholic Church)
